title: Logos & Branding
sources: README.md: Grid structure and Matériel action definition [cite: 2]
quests.ts: Foundational Quest Spec for A6
forom-config.ts: Default category and question labels [cite: 3]
memories.ts: Memory data structure and helper functions
database.sql: PostgreSQL schema for memos table
App.tsx: Pixel economy and quest reward logic
resume: The quest "Logos & Branding" (A6) is situated at the intersection of the Vision district and the Matériel action within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid[cite: 2]. In the Forom ecosystem, the "Matériel" tag is specifically reserved for the management and archival of community assets, files, and visual identity[cite: 2, 3]. As part of Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest involves the creation and storage of the graphical elements that define the community's public and private face[cite: 2]. This includes logos, color palettes, and branding guidelines that align with the community's core mission, such as the Public Forom's mission to "Save human communities & long-term memory"[cite: 2]. Technically, these assets are referenced within a "memo" stored in the local PostgreSQL database, where the question field is set to '6' (Matériel) and the category_id corresponds to Vision[cite: 3]. The description field of the memo, which supports extensive text, can be used to document the design rationale and usage rules for these assets. The modern frontend stack, including Tailwind 4 and Vite 7, allows for the efficient rendering of these visual assets within the spatial 5x5 viewport of the FRM grid[cite: 2]. For the "Creator" role—those users aligned with innovation and production—completing this quest is a vital contribution that pushes the community's mission forward[cite: 2]. Upon successful submission, the contributor is rewarded with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, reinforcing the community's redistribution of purchasing power[cite: 2]. This quest is essential for establishing a cohesive identity before the community moves into Phase 2 (Iteration) and eventually Phase 3 (Awakening), where the local RAG system, ROM, will be trained on this archived data[cite: 2]. By hosting these branding assets locally on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community ensures total data sovereignty and long-term preservation of its visual history[cite: 2]. This is particularly critical for pilot projects like the ETS Clubs, where branding ensures continuity and recognition across student generations[cite: 2]. The resulting memo acts as a permanent record in the V1 Archive, providing the visual DNA that will be refined and canonized in subsequent phases of the ROMAP[cite: 2]. Ultimately, A6 transforms abstract vision into a tangible, recognizable brand that secures the community’s professional presence within the global forom lobby once connectivity is earned[cite: 2].
summary: This quest facilitates the creation and archival of community branding assets at coordinate A6, securing the visual identity required for long-term memory and professional recognition.
